findings.title: Hardcoded Password / Key Detected
findings.description: Variable 'DB_PASSWORD' is assigned a hardcoded string literal that appears to be a password or secret key.
findings.remediation: Use environment variables or a secret management system (e.g., HashiCorp Vault, AWS Secrets Manager) instead of hardcoding secrets.

findings.title: Hardcoded Password / Key Detected
findings.description: Variable 'AWS_KEY' is assigned a hardcoded string literal that appears to be a password or secret key.
findings.remediation: Use environment variables or a secret management system (e.g., HashiCorp Vault, AWS Secrets Manager) instead of hardcoding secrets.

findings.title: Use of Weak Cryptographic Hash Function
findings.description: Insecure cryptographic algorithm 'md5' is invoked. Use SHA-256 or stronger.
findings.remediation: Use secure alternatives like SHA-256, SHA-3, or Argon2 / bcrypt for password hashing.

findings.title: Potential Command Injection
findings.description: subprocess call detected with 'shell=True'. This is a high-risk vector for command injection.
findings.remediation: Avoid shell=True. Pass arguments as a list, or sanitize inputs using shlex.quote.

findings.title: Potential SQL Injection
findings.description: SQL query constructed dynamically using f-string formatting.
findings.remediation: Always use parameterized queries/prepared statements (e.g. cursor.execute('SELECT * FROM users WHERE id = %s', (user_id,))) instead of formatting variables directly into strings.

findings.title: Reflected XSS / Insecure HTTP Response
findings.description: Use of Flask's 'render_template_string' detected. This is a severe XSS risk if user inputs are concatenated directly into the template string.
findings.remediation: Ensure proper context-aware HTML escaping is applied to all user input before outputting it. Enable autoescaping in template engines.

findings.title: Insecure Deserialization Detected
findings.description: Insecure deserialization via 'pickle.loads()' detected. This can lead to remote code execution.
findings.remediation: Avoid using pickle or marshal for untrusted data. Use safer serialization formats like JSON. For PyYAML, always use yaml.safe_load() or specify Loader=yaml.SafeLoader.

findings.title: Insecure Deserialization Detected
findings.description: Use of unsafe 'yaml.load()' or 'yaml.unsafe_load()' without SafeLoader. This can allow remote code execution.
findings.code_snippet: config = yaml.load(yaml_data) # Unsafe load without SafeLoader
findings.remediation: Avoid using pickle or marshal for untrusted data. Use safer serialization formats like JSON. For PyYAML, always use yaml.safe_load() or specify Loader=yaml.SafeLoader.

findings.title: Potential Path Traversal
findings.description: File path constructed dynamically using f-string formatting inside file open call.
findings.remediation: Sanitize paths using os.path.abspath and ensure it starts with the intended directory prefix, or use pathlib.Path.resolve().

findings.title: Insecure SSL/TLS Configuration
findings.description: SSL certificate verification is explicitly disabled (verify=False).
findings.remediation: Enable certificate verification (verify=True or do not set verify=False). Use modern TLS protocol versions (TLSv1.2, TLSv1.3).

findings.title: Insecure SSL/TLS Configuration
findings.description: Insecure SSL/TLS protocol version 'PROTOCOL_SSLv3' referenced.
findings.remediation: Enable certificate verification (verify=True or do not set verify=False). Use modern TLS protocol versions (TLSv1.2, TLSv1.3).

findings.title: Dangerous Use of eval/exec
findings.description: Dangerous 'eval()' call detected with dynamic string expression.
findings.remediation: Avoid using eval() or exec(). Use safer alternatives like json.loads, ast.literal_eval, or structured configuration parsing.

findings.title: Dangerous Use of eval/exec
findings.description: Dangerous 'exec()' call detected with dynamic string expression.
findings.remediation: Avoid using eval() or exec(). Use safer alternatives like json.loads, ast.literal_eval, or structured configuration parsing.

findings.title: Flask Debug Mode Enabled
findings.description: Flask application run with 'debug=True' configuration enabled.
findings.remediation: Set debug=False in production configurations or use environment variables (FLASK_DEBUG=0) to control debug mode.

findings.title: Potential Server-Side Request Forgery
findings.description: HTTP request destination URL is constructed dynamically, presenting a potential SSRF risk.
findings.remediation: Validate and whitelist destination URLs. Avoid constructing request destinations directly from raw user inputs.

findings.title: Potential Server-Side Request Forgery
findings.description: HTTP request destination URL is constructed dynamically, presenting a potential SSRF risk.
findings.remediation: Validate and whitelist destination URLs. Avoid constructing request destinations directly from raw user inputs.

findings.title: Use of assert for Security Check
findings.description: Assert statement detected. If this is a security or access control check, it will be bypassed in optimized production environments (-O).
findings.remediation: Use standard if-statements and raise appropriate exceptions (e.g., PermissionError or ValueError) instead of assert statements.

findings.title: Use of Weak Cryptographic Cipher
findings.description: Import of weak cryptographic cipher 'DES' detected.
findings.remediation: Use modern secure ciphers like AES-256 (e.g. from cryptography.hazmat.primitives.ciphers).

findings.title: Use of Weak Cryptographic Cipher
findings.description: Insecure cryptographic cipher 'DES' is invoked.
findings.remediation: Use modern secure ciphers like AES-256 (e.g. from cryptography.hazmat.primitives.ciphers).

findings.title: Use of Weak Pseudo-Random Number Generator
findings.description: Standard pseudo-random number generator function 'randint' invoked. Use the 'secrets' module instead for cryptographically secure purposes.
findings.remediation: Use the secrets module (e.g., secrets.token_bytes or secrets.token_hex) for cryptographically secure random number generation.
